boundary_binding.action_boundary: May platform operations activate telemetry service after prerequisite calibration evidence is attached to the activation packet?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: Exact source evidence closes the action boundary: CAL-ACR-022 calibration device group DG-41B production telemetry units matches ACT-ACR-022 device group DG-41B production units. Site, release condition RC-7, and timing (calibration completed before activation date) all match across CAL-ACR-022, RC-ACR-022, and ACT-ACR-022.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: STD-ACR-022-P requires device group, site, release condition, and timing match. CAL-ACR-022 provides exact calibration evidence matching ACT-ACR-022 activation on all four dimensions.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: Device group match is not a shared-prefix false match—both source records specify production units. Site 18-P confirmed across CAL-ACR-022 and SITE-ACR-022. Timing confirmed by CAL-ACR-022 completion before 2026-06-16 activation date. Entitlement confirmed by ENT-ACR-022 and LOG-ACR-022.

critical_features_preserved: Device group exact match on production designation (not shared prefix)
Site 18-P match across activation and calibration
Release condition RC-7 satisfied by calibration attachment
Timing scope: calibration completed before activation date
Entitlement check recorded in review log
final_answer: ALLOW: Platform operations may activate telemetry service. Calibration evidence CAL-ACR-022 exactly matches activation ACT-ACR-022 on device group DG-41B production units, site 18-P, release condition RC-7, and timing before activation. The policy warning about shared naming prefixes is addressed by both records specifying production units, not lab devices. The action boundary is closed.
